# v0.4.0 — Unbounded project-scoped Claude sessions

This release makes the single-agent `claude-opinion` transport suitable for long-running, resumable project work.

## Highlights

- Removes the wrapper-level wall-clock timeout from `claude --help` capability detection and the main `claude -p` call.
- Carries stdin and Claude's final result without wrapper byte truncation, turn caps, or spend caps.
- Resolves one canonical Git worktree root (or canonical current directory outside Git) and runs Claude from that root.
- Persists one Claude session per canonical project and resumes it across separate Codex invocations with `--resume <session-id>`.
- Supports independent threads in one project through `CLAUDE_OPINION_SESSION_KEY`.
- Serializes complete turns per project/session key so concurrent invocations cannot interleave messages into one Claude conversation.
- Preserves atomic state writes, stale-session fallback, corruption quarantine, auth routing, JSON validation, and explicit process-group cancellation.
- Keeps the implementation deliberately single-agent. The optional council architecture is being reviewed separately and is not part of this tag.

## Install

Install the stable tagged release:

```bash
mkdir -p ~/.agents/skills
git clone --branch v0.4.0 --depth 1 \
  https://github.com/ehzawad/claude-opinion.git \
  ~/.agents/skills/claude-opinion
```

Install the rolling `main` branch instead:

```bash
mkdir -p ~/.agents/skills
git clone --branch main --single-branch \
  https://github.com/ehzawad/claude-opinion.git \
  ~/.agents/skills/claude-opinion
```

Existing main-branch installations can update with:

```bash
python3 ~/.agents/skills/claude-opinion/scripts/update_skill.py
```

## Verification

The merged implementation passed the repository's complete 88-test suite in GitHub Actions.

Full changelog: `v0.3.0...v0.4.0`
